role: A generic, declarative React panel renderer that turns a panel definition (title + fields) into editable controls, isolated so render-time errors are caught by its own boundary.
capabilities: Render a titled panel from a declarative `p` object
Map a list of fields into labeled controls
Render fallback `input` text fields with blur-to-commit
Show a 'no fields' placeholder when the field list is empty
substance_why: It only supports `select` and a generic text `input`, with no validation, typed inputs, dynamic options, or field metadata beyond label/target/type/options.
interactions_missing: No validation or error feedback on user input
No support for number, checkbox, textarea, or custom field types
No handling of empty/invalid options in selects
No keyboard handling (e.g. Enter to commit)
No loading or async state for values
performance_risks: `value(f)` is invoked twice per field per render (for `value` prop and `defaultValue`)
Inline arrow functions for `onChange`/`onBlur` are recreated on every render, causing child select/input re-renders
reuse_why: The declarative panel-rendering concept is worth keeping, but it needs typed schemas, controlled inputs, and per-field components to fit a redesigned interface.
decomposition_hints: Extract a `Field` subcomponent to encapsulate select/input rendering and local change handling
Split field-type variants (SelectField, TextField) for future extension
Separate the panel schema/types into a dedicated model file
Move value formatting/commit logic out of the renderer into a hook or controller
